own_review.point: Docs claim constructors are checked ('This check reads method & constructor names' in checkstyle-metadata.properties; isForbiddenMethod javadoc says 'method/constructor call') but only TokenTypes.METHOD_CALL is registered — LITERAL_NEW, CTOR_CALL, SUPER_CTOR_CALL and METHOD_REF are never visited; doc/behavior mismatch

Argument counting via dot.getNextSibling().getChildCount(TokenTypes.EXPR) undercounts: verified with checkstyle 8.8 AST that a LAMBDA argument is a direct ELIST child (not wrapped in EXPR), so forEach(x -> ...) counts 0 args and foo(1, x->x, ref) counts 2 — forbidden methods taking lambda args are matched against the wrong count
IntRange javadoc is stale/wrong: fields are primitive int but comments say 'No lower limit when null' and constructor params documented 'null is equivalent to 0/infinity' — null is impossible for int

setArgumentCount mutates state before validation completes: this.argumentCount is assigned first and argumentCountRanges.clear() runs before parsing every token, so an invalid later token (e.g. '2, badStart-badEnd') leaves the check in a partially-updated state; validate fully, then swap

IntRange.from declares throws CheckstyleException but a dash-free non-numeric token ('badArgCount') or out-of-int value escapes as raw NumberFormatException from Integer.parseInt — inconsistent error contract (only works because beanutils/reflection wraps any exception into 'Cannot set property')

qualitative: The human reviewer approved with no findings, so almost everything here had to be verified directly against the diff — and all four candidates fabricated heavily, apparently reviewing an imagined version of the PR. Three of four (v1, v2/v3 partly) invented the same phantom blocker (missing forbid.certain.method key) even though the diff plainly adds it, and v2/v4 invented nonexistent helper APIs (parseIntegerInterval, parseArgumentCount, IntegerInterval) while v2 also declared three used imports unused and the present Sonar entry missing. v4 is the least bad: it found the one genuine doc/behavior mismatch (constructors claimed but only METHOD_CALL registered) and a real, verifiable description-formatting defect that exceeds both the human and my review, against five fabrications. v3 also caught the constructor mismatch but everything else it said was false; v1 and v2 contributed zero true findings. No candidate found the genuine correctness bug — lambda arguments are not wrapped in EXPR in the checkstyle AST, so getChildCount(TokenTypes.EXPR) undercounts arguments — nor the stale IntRange null-javadoc or the partial-state setter.
